rationale: The cited item is a non-empirical JMR editorial on field-level reproducibility and practice-relevance standards, while the citing paper’s core contribution is an empirical decoy-effect replication program and its stated prior-work linkage emphasizes Frederick et al. (2014), not this editorial. With only a single DOI match and no in-text citation context, this looks plausibly background framing but not clearly enough to safely drop.
